metadata.notes: Vibe adapter now declares Control{SupportsInput:true,Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC,Presets:{compact:/compact}} + ControlPermission() (agent.go, fix 5e22e47) -> daemon offers backchannel input/interrupt/compact into the vibe REPL via its terminal backend; live drive confirmed at record time.

assessment.body:
## Verdict: agent=yes, daemon=full, driver=ready

**agent=yes.** Mistral Vibe (v2.19.0) is an interactive Textual TUI/REPL: `VibeApp(App)` in `vibe/cli/textual_ui/app.py` reads typed input through a `Prompt` widget, dispatches `SlashCommand`/`UserCommandMessage`, and buffers turns via `MessageQueue` (message_queue.py) with a `terminal_input_filter.py`. It is therefore drivable by injected keystrokes delivered into its controlling terminal (tmux/kitty/iTerm2/Terminal.app), exactly the backchannel-control mechanism, plus slash presets like /compact.

**daemon=full (was bug).** The prior verdict was `daemon=bug` because `core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/agent.go` declared NO `Control` (zero value -> SupportsInput=false), so the daemon derived vibe sessions as non-controllable and offered no backchannel input/interrupt/preset. Commit 5e22e47 FIXES this: the adapter now declares `Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:agent.InterruptCtrlC, Presets:{backchannel.PresetCompact: "/compact"}}` and adds `agent.ControlPermission()` to `Permissions` (agent.go:40-48). The behavioral test `TestAgent_Control_Backchannel` (adapter_test.go:50) asserts all three axes plus the presence of the shared control consent gate. With Control declared, the shared control seam applies unchanged: InputService gate (backchannel toggle -> control consent -> controllable), Controller tmux/kitty injectors keyed off the Launcher, `/compact` preset, and Ctrl-C interrupt. The new `modify`-kind ControlPermission is the consent boundary (contracttesting.AssertPermissionGated, core/internal/contracttesting/permission_gate.go). No spec-required observable is mis-handled -> `daemon=full`.

**driver=ready.** `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` implements every step the recipe needs — `send`, `slash`, `interrupt`, `sleep`, `wait_turn` are all present. No `gap:*`.

Route: record — driver ready and daemon full; the recording drives a real backchannel input (ready -> working on injected text), an interrupt (settles back to ready), and the /compact preset. Live backchannel drive is confirmed at record time.
caveats: Emission settled at record: that injected keystrokes actually drive vibe's Textual TUI to a new turn (and Ctrl-C interrupts it) is confirmed by the live drive that record performs; confidence 0.85 pending that recording promoting provisional -> settled.
Vibe default model not pinned in the spec observations (not confirmed from the live surface); record fills it from the recording.

sources.note: fix 5e22e47: adapter now declares Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC, Presets:{compact:/compact}} (lines 40-46) + agent.ControlPermission() (line 48) -> daemon offers backchannel control
TestAgent_Control_Backchannel (line 50) asserts SupportsInput, Interrupt==InterruptCtrlC, Presets[compact]==/compact, and ControlPermission() present
Control{SupportsInput,Interrupt,Presets} axis, InterruptCtrlC, and ControlPermission() helper (KindModify)
AssertPermissionGated — verify contract for the modify-kind ControlPermission the Control declaration adds
VibeApp(App) Textual TUI/REPL: Prompt widget, SlashCommand/UserCommandMessage dispatch -> reads typed terminal input (agent=yes)

script.text: Write a haiku about a lighthouse, then wait for my next message.
Now write a second haiku about the sea.
